memory.summary: I'm stressed about the project but my team needs me; I'll skip the quick chore to hang out with cohort_3 instead.

desires: Protect my bond with cohort_3 by ignoring the trivial chore and staying present with them
Vent my stress immediately rather than planning for the project due in three days
Avoid feeling guilty about neglecting the quick win if it means losing connection with my friends
Get a quick emotional boost from the group right now instead of worrying about tomorrow's sleep debt

goals.description: Stay with cohort_3 and attend the leisure event immediately to reinforce our bond.
goals.rationale: My core value is relationships (0.5). Ignoring a trivial chore to protect my closeness with my friends aligns perfectly with my affiliation axis. I'm impulsive, so I'm choosing the immediate emotional boost over the future project deadline.

goals.description: Vent stress and complain about the upcoming project deadline while hanging out.
goals.rationale: I need to assert my feelings right now rather than suppressing them to plan ahead. Venting maintains my authentic connection with the group and releases current tension.

goals.description: Ignore the quick_chore and the term project for the rest of the afternoon.
goals.rationale: Tasks are secondary to people. Since I'm prioritizing the 'quick win' of social connection, I will defer the easy chore and the soft-difficulty project. The guilt isn't worth the isolation.

agent.last_reasoning: Advances g1 (bonding with cohort_3) by prioritizing immediate relationships over the deferred project. I'm impulsive and value connection above tasks, so I'll skip the easy chore to hang out now.
